creator: kbowling@freebsd.org
text:
From Ted Unangst:  "The Firefox issue is because gtk is brain damaged and sends the image to the x server, then reads it back, but it gets broken up into tiny chunks and causes a billion syscalls. You can fix it by recompiling x with larger socket buffers."

OpenBSD did a Xenocara patch https://freedesktop.org/patch/23215/
